content_markdown: A substance in the gas state has a density about 1000 times less than when it is in the liquid state. The diameter of a molecule is $d$. What is the best estimate of the average distance between molecules in the gas state?
vibe_explanation: Density is inversely proportional to volume. If the density of the gas is 1000 times less than the liquid, the volume occupied per molecule in the gas is 1000 times greater than in the liquid. Since volume scales with the cube of the linear distance ($V \propto r^3$), the average distance between molecules increases by the cube root of the volume factor. The cube root of 1000 is 10, so the distance becomes $10d$.
